name: config
context: Write, validate, and reload nginx configuration files — server blocks, locations, and directives
info: This skill covers the nginx configuration language: writing server blocks, location matching, directive syntax, and config management. Agents use this to create virtual host configurations, set up static file serving, define location routing rules, configure logging, and manage the config reload lifecycle. Config files live in /etc/nginx/ and use a hierarchical context model (main → http → server → location). Always validate with `nginx -t` before reloading. The skill includes creating new site configs in conf.d/ or sites-available/, enabling/disabling sites, and understanding directive inheritance between contexts. See [[nginx/reverse-proxy]] for proxy_pass setup and [[nginx/ssl]] for HTTPS configuration.

sections.title: Server blocks and virtual hosts
sections.body: Each `server` block defines a virtual host identified by `listen` (port) and `server_name` (hostname). Nginx selects the matching server block by: 1) matching the listen address:port, then 2) matching the Host header against server_name values. If no server_name matches, the `default_server` block handles the request. Use `server_name _` as a catch-all. Multiple server_name values are space-separated; wildcards (`*.example.com`) and regex (`~^(?<sub>.+)\.example\.com$`) are supported.

sections.title: Location blocks and URI matching
sections.body: Location blocks route requests by URI. Matching order: exact (`=`) → prefix with `^~` → regex (`~` / `~*`) → longest prefix. Inside locations, `try_files` checks paths in order: `try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html` tries the exact file, then a directory, then falls back to index.html (common for SPAs). Use `internal` to make a location only accessible from nginx itself (error_page, X-Accel-Redirect). Nested locations inherit the parent's context.

sections.title: Access and error logging
sections.body: Configure logging with `access_log` and `error_log`. Define custom formats with `log_format` using variables like `$remote_addr`, `$request`, `$status`, `$body_bytes_sent`, `$http_referer`, `$http_user_agent`, `$upstream_response_time`. Error log levels: `debug`, `info`, `notice`, `warn`, `error`, `crit`, `alert`, `emerg`. Disable access logging per-location with `access_log off`. For JSON logs: `log_format json escape=json '{"remote_addr":"$remote_addr",...}'`.

sections.title: Performance tuning directives
sections.body:
Key performance directives:
- `worker_processes auto` — one worker per CPU core
- `worker_connections 1024` — max connections per worker (total = workers × connections)
- `sendfile on` — kernel-level file transfer (avoids user-space copy)
- `tcp_nopush on` — send headers and file in one packet
- `tcp_nodelay on` — disable Nagle's algorithm for keepalive
- `keepalive_timeout 65` — how long idle connections stay open
- `gzip on` + `gzip_types text/plain application/json ...` — compress responses
- `client_max_body_size 10m` — max upload body size (default 1m)
- `open_file_cache max=1000 inactive=20s` — cache file descriptors

sections.title: Includes and config snippets
sections.body:
Use `include` to split config into manageable files:
- `include /etc/nginx/conf.d/*.conf` — all .conf files in directory
- `include /etc/nginx/snippets/ssl-params.conf` — reusable SSL config
- `include /etc/nginx/mime.types` — MIME type mappings

Create reusable snippets for proxy headers, SSL settings, and security headers. Include them in server blocks to avoid duplication. Globs are supported: `include /etc/nginx/conf.d/*.conf` loads all matching files in alphabetical order.

SITE_NAME.description: Site name for the configuration file (e.g., myapp)

ROOT_DIR.description: Document root for static file serving

backend_url.description: Backend URL for reverse proxy (omit for static sites)

config_path.description: Path to the generated config file
